scope_note: Harvard's admissions site publishes the current testing policy and Class of 2029 application counts, while the most detailed official SAT/ACT percentile ranges appear in Harvard's 2023-24 Common Data Set for enrolled first-year students. Harvard does not publish testing ranges by major.
policy: Harvard requires the SAT or ACT. In exceptional access cases, applicants may satisfy the testing requirement with AP, IB, GCSE/A-Level, or national leaving exam results or predictions.

claims.claim: Harvard requires the SAT or ACT to meet its standardized testing requirement.

In exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible, Harvard says AP exam results, IB actual or predicted scores, GCSE/A-Level actual or predicted results, or national leaving exam results or predictions can meet the requirement.
Harvard says there are no test-score cutoffs and that it does not admit “by the numbers.”
Applicants may self-report SAT or ACT scores, and enrolling students later submit official scores.
